steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A line with slope $m$ and y-intercept $b$ has equation $y=mx+b$. The graph shows a y-intercept of $7$, so $b=7$.

steps.title: Substitute the slope
steps.content:
The slope is $\frac{1}{5}$, so the equation is:
$$f(x)=\frac{1}{5}x+7$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A — $f \left(x\right)=\frac{1}{5}x+7$ matches both the slope and intercept. Choice B — $f \left(x\right)=\frac{1}{5}x+\frac{1}{7}$ has the wrong intercept, Choice C — $f(x)=\frac{1}{5}x-\frac{7}{5}$ has intercept $-\frac{7}{5}$, and Choice D — $f \left(x\right)=\frac{1}{5}x-7$ has intercept $-7$. Choice A — $f \left(x\right)=\frac{1}{5}x+7$.
